_status: SIGNED-OFF 2026-07-12 — owner-authorized go-live (full batch). Spring River (AR), spring_fed_float. Reach 1 (Dam 3→Hardy) on AGFC typical discharge (07069305, cfs): optimal 206–694. [verify] cleared (07069305, 07069500); McIlroy access law. FOLLOW-UP: dangerous anchor still unsourced (NWS flood stages over-anchored: 10 ft = 15,868 cfs, ~23x floatable) — outfitter call is the path (RiverStop 870-955-5088). Lower reach uncalibrated. Access points approximate (interpolation), flagged for verification.
name: Spring River
difficultyRating: Class I-II (Saddler Falls & High Falls ledges)
description: A cold, clear, spring-fed float in north-central Arkansas rising at Mammoth Spring (one of the largest springs in the U.S.) and running past Hardy toward its confluence with the Black River near Black Rock. Reliable year-round flow from the spring; trout in the upper reach, smallmouth lower; a few ledges/rapids including Saddler Falls. A very popular canoe/kayak float.
managingAuthority: Arkansas Game & Fish Commission (public accesses); Arkansas State Parks (Mammoth Spring State Park); streambed navigability per Arkansas law

accessLaw.value: Arkansas follows the recreational (public-use) navigability doctrine: the public may float and wade streams susceptible to recreational use by small craft for a substantial portion of the year; everything below the ordinary high-water mark is public, but riparian owners may bar crossing their land, so lawful entry is via public accesses (AGFC Dam 3, Bayou, Cold Springs; Mammoth Spring State Park). Adopted in State v. McIlroy, 268 Ark. 227 (1980); see Ark. Code Ann. § 22-6-201.
accessLaw.source: State v. McIlroy, 268 Ark. 227 (1980); Ark. Code Ann. § 22-6-201

gauges.name: Spring River at Spring Street Bridge at Hardy, AR
gauges.knownBias: downstream end of the reach (drainage 845 vs ~280 at the spring) — reads higher than the Dam 3 put-in

sections.name: Mammoth Spring / Dam 3 → Hardy (~15 mi)
sections.description: The classic Spring River float: put in at Dam 3 / Riverside (~3.2 mi below the spring, the main put-in) and take out at Hardy, ~15 mi. Fed by Mammoth Spring's constant ~340 cfs, 58°F baseflow, so it runs cold, clear, and floatable year-round and is effectively never too low (no too_low anchor set). AGFC-stocked trout water in the cold upper stretch; Class I-II with ledges (Saddler Falls, High Falls) and low-head Dams #1 and #3 to portage in high water.

representativeGauge.rationale: Spring River at Hardy (07069305) is the only reach gauge reporting both discharge (00060) and stage (00065) live, sits at the reach take-out, and carries an NWS forecast point (HDYA4); AGFC's typical-discharge range is quoted in cfs, matching this gauge's 00060.
representativeGauge.knownBias: at the downstream (Hardy) end of the reach — drainage 845 sqmi vs ~280 at the spring/Dam 3 put-in, so it reads higher than the upper put-in.

thresholds.source: Arkansas Game & Fish Commission Spring River trout page: typical discharge 206–694 cfs (https://www.agfc.com/fishing/where-to-fish/trout-waters/spring-river/)
AGFC Spring River: typical discharge top of range 694 cfs

publishedFloatTimes.source: spring-river-findings-partial.md — classic Dam 3 → Hardy float ≈ 15 mi (typical ~6–7 hr outfitter estimate; confirm with RiverStop / River Wilderness Sports)

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: spring-fed — rarely too low to float
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: portage Saddler Falls & High Falls in high water; cold 58°F water year-round (hypothermia)

hazards.value: Saddler Falls — Class II ledge / S-turn at ~river mile 5; scout or portage in high water
High Falls — ~6-ft waterfall / ledge; portage in high water
Low-head Dam #1 (Mammoth Spring SP / Spring Lake) and Dam #3 (~3.2 mi below the spring, at the main put-in) — low-head-dam drowning hazard; keep clear / portage
Low-water bridge on the Many Islands → Hardy stretch — portage

localKnowledge: AGFC-stocked trout water from Dam #1 down to Myatt Creek (~12 mi) — put-and-take rainbow and brown trout in the cold upper reach.
Extremely popular summer party float (canoes, kayaks, tubes); heavy weekend crowds Memorial Day–Labor Day, with outfitters concentrated around Hardy and Mammoth Spring.

sections.name: Hardy → Ravenden → Imboden → Black River
sections.description: The lower Spring River from Hardy down through Ravenden and Imboden to the Black River confluence near Black Rock. Larger, warmer, and far less calibrated than the upper trout reach; smallmouth, walleye, and tiger-muskie fishery. Spring-fed baseflow keeps it floatable, but no gauge-keyed floatability thresholds were captured — thresholds are intentionally left empty pending Imboden 07069500 calibration.

takeOut.name: Imboden (to Black River confluence near Black Rock)

representativeGauge.rationale: Spring River at Imboden (07069500) is the long-record (since 1936) discharge gauge on the lower river and the only pollable gauge below Hardy representing the Hardy → Imboden → Black River reach.
representativeGauge.knownBias: UNCALIBRATED — near the lower end of the reach (drainage 1180 sqmi), reads higher than the Hardy end; no floatability thresholds captured, so thresholds are intentionally empty pending Imboden-keyed data.

localKnowledge: Lower-river smallmouth, walleye, and tiger-muskie fishery; much less commercial float traffic than the upper trout reach.

accessPoints.name: Mammoth Spring State Park (Dam #1)
accessPoints.notes: The source — Mammoth Spring (one of the largest springs in the U.S.) and low-head Dam #1 / Spring Lake in Mammoth Spring State Park; uppermost access. GPS PENDING [manual].
accessPoints.source: spring-river-findings-partial.md (AGFC 2009 Spring River access map; Arkansas State Parks)

accessPoints.name: Cold Springs Access
accessPoints.notes: AGFC access ~river mile 1; ADA fishing pier. GPS PENDING [manual].

accessPoints.name: Lassiter Access
accessPoints.notes: Walk-in access; river mile and ownership unverified. GPS PENDING [manual].

accessPoints.name: Dam 3 / Riverside Access
accessPoints.notes: MAIN put-in for the classic Dam 3 → Hardy float, ~3.2 mi below the spring, just below low-head Dam #3. GPS PENDING [manual].

accessPoints.name: Hardy (Spring Street Bridge)
accessPoints.notes: Standard take-out for the Dam 3 → Hardy float (~15 river mi below Dam 3) at the Spring Street bridge; co-located with USGS gauge 07069305; also the put-in for the lower reach. Coordinate sourced from the USGS gauge location; ownership unverified.

accessPoints.name: Many Islands
accessPoints.notes: Private Many Islands Camp & Canoe (60+ yr) upstream of Hardy; a low-water bridge lies on the Many Islands → Hardy stretch (portage). Private — not a public access. GPS PENDING [manual].

accessPoints.name: Ravenden Access
accessPoints.notes: Lower-river access at Ravenden (Lawrence Co.) on the Hardy → Imboden reach; type/ownership unverified. GPS PENDING [manual].

accessPoints.name: Imboden Access
accessPoints.notes: Lower-river access at Imboden, near USGS gauge 07069500 and above the Black River confluence; type/ownership unverified. GPS PENDING [manual].

openQuestions: NWS-FLOOD DANGER ANCHOR INVALID (checked 2026-07-12 vs owner-provided HDYA4 flood impacts): the Hardy gauge flood onset (10 ft = banks breaking) = 15,868 cfs on the USGS 07069305 exsa rating — ~23x the AGFC floatable max (694 cfs); access/campground flooding impacts start even higher (12-14 ft). Same over-anchoring as Gasconade — do NOT use NWS flood stage as the dangerous anchor. The outfitter call (RiverStop 870-955-5088 / River Wilderness 870-955-5003) remains the only valid path to a floater do-not-float number.

DANGEROUS ANCHOR UNSOURCED (reach 1): no outfitter/AGFC/American Whitewater do-not-float cfs; outfitters run 'by judgment.' Call RiverStop (870-955-5088) and River Wilderness Sports (870-955-5003) to source a floater-danger number at Hardy 07069305. NWS HDYA4 action 8 / minor-flood 10 ft is valley flooding, NOT floater danger — do not use as the dangerous anchor. Until sourced, optimal_max (694 cfs) drives the high band; no dangerous anchor is set.

Representative-gauge choice per reach [signoff]: reach 1 (Dam 3 → Hardy) on Hardy 07069305 (00060+00065 live, NWS point); reach 2 (Hardy → Imboden → Black River) on Imboden 07069500. Confirm the app polls the right gauge for each reach via river mile.

AGFC DATUM RECONCILIATION: AGFC's 'typical 4.0–5.3 ft' stage does NOT match the Hardy gauge (443 cfs read at 2.96 ft on 2026-07-12) — the AGFC ft figure is a different gauge/datum. Thresholds are encoded in AGFC cfs (206–694), NOT its ft. Reconcile the ft datum before publishing any stage-based guidance.

LOWER REACH UNCALIBRATED (spring-reach2): no Imboden-keyed floatability thresholds captured; thresholds intentionally left empty. Capture optimal/high anchors at 07069500 before the lower reach can drive a badge.
toVerify: USGS 07069305 (Spring River at Hardy, Spring St Bridge) and 07069500 (Spring River at Imboden): site #, name, lat/lon, params (00060/00065), drainage area transcribed to verified-identifiers-spring-river.md (written). 07069220 near Mammoth Spring EXCLUDED — 00060 discharge discontinued 2016 (ft only), so it is not in gauges[].

Hardy HUC anomaly: the USGS Hardy site page reports HUC 11010008 while NHD/NHDPlus_HR gives 11010010 for the flowline — the agent corrected the brief's 11010012 to 11010010. Reconcile the Hardy-site HUC vs NHD before ingest.
Single NHD Permanent Identifier for the flowline (multi-segment; no single PID captured). GNIS feature id 00055122 is on hand — resolve the NHDPlus_HR flowline PID(s) in AR HUC 11010010.

Access-point GPS [manual]: only the Hardy take-out (36.3136, -91.4828) is sourced; a human must place coordinates for Mammoth Spring SP/Dam 1, Cold Springs, Lassiter, Dam 3/Riverside, Bayou, Many Islands (private), Ravenden, and Imboden (AGFC 2009 map gives river-miles, not GPS).
River length conflict: 57 mi (Wikipedia) vs ~75 mi (Encyclopedia of Arkansas). Resolve the total mainstem length; the calibrated float (Dam 3 → Hardy) is ~15 mi regardless.
AR navigability doctrine RESOLVED for the state: State v. McIlroy, 268 Ark. 227 (1980) + Ark. Code Ann. § 22-6-201 — accessLaw upgraded to high confidence and reusable across AR rivers.
